Motion For Protective Order, Motion For Sanctions, And Motion To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TRAVIS J. SIEGEL, Esq. of Valentine Law Group, APC (Tel: 949-265-9272) for plaintiff;
Attys. DEXTER B. LOUIE, Esq. and ERIK S. FAUSSNER, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (Tel: 415-288-9800) for defendant John W. Pierce, M.D.;
Atty. D. SCOTT BARBER, Esq. of Wilson Getty LLP (Tel: 858-847-3237) for defendants Victorian Post Acute; Providence Group, Inc., Providence Administrative Consulting Services, Inc., and Bay Area Master Tenant, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS YI LI, LI YUN WANG, and MARY CHEUNG's Motion For Protective Order, Motion For Sanctions, And Motion To Compel Deposition.

Parties to the motion previously stipulated to the pro tem judge at a prior hearing.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification as follows.

The motion is granted. Dr. Pierce shall appear at a third session of his deposition. (1) In response to deposition questions, Defense Counsel will make only good faith objections in a non-suggestive manner and (2) Defense Counsel will not make speaking objections or otherwise comment on the pending question unless Plaintiff's Counsel asks for clarification of the objection. Although not every objection of Dr. Pierce's counsel was improper, he made multiple improper speaking objections, multiple objections that were clearly without merit, and multiple comments that could have suggested the answer to the witness. This must cease. A separate statement is unnecessary and impractical for this motion since it is not a motion to compel answers to specific questions to which objections were made.

Sanctions are denied. The court feels it would be unjust to award sanctions on this motion. Although the court finds the meet and confer passes, it definitely could have been better. The court hopes that hereafter, plaintiff's counsel meets and confers after the deposition and not just during it, before filing a motion. Additionally, plaintiff does complain about some objections that were proper, this appears to be the first motion of this type in the case, and possibly Dr. Pierce's counsel in his advocate's zeal in the deposition didn't entirely perceive the impropriety of some of his conduct. This order provides him with notice not to engage in such conduct in the future.

Dr. Pierce's deposition is to take place by March 24, 2022. If a conflict arises with Dr. Pierce's schedule, the parties may request an extension of this deadline by way of an email sent to the Judge Pro Tem.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and then email the proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Mary Ferreira, CSR No. 10553, Email: mferreira@calreporter.net, Tel: 510-928-6268 (302/JPT)

Atty. D. SCOTT BARBER, Esq. of Wilson Getty LLP (Tel: 858-847-3237) for defendants Victorian Post Acute; Providence Group, Inc., Providence Administrative Consulting Services, Inc., and Bay Area Master Tenant, LLC.

Hearing Re: Defendant John W. Pierce, M.D.'s Motion For Protective Order.

Parties to the motion previously stipulated to the pro tem judge at a prior hearing.
Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification as follows.

The motion is denied. The parties agreed that Dr. Pierce would appear for questioning on the matters alleged in the medical board cases. Additionally, within the broad scope of relevancy in discovery, the prior matters are relevant to Dr. Pierce's state of mind, whether he acted with reckless or malicious intent, and whether he had knowledge of the alleged dangerous consequences of his actions. Among other things poor recordkeeping and failure to properly diagnose and/or treat pancreatitis are alleged in the medical board matters and in this case. The fact that the second medical board accusation was issued after the events of this case occurred does not make the accusation irrelevant for discovery purposes. Among other things, Dr. Pierce knew when the events of this case occurred that the Medical Board was investigating him and he most probably knew what he had done and hadn't done and what had happened to his patients in the prior matters.

Dr. Pierce's deposition is to take place by March 24, 2022. If a conflict arises with Dr. Pierce's schedule, the parties may request an extension of this deadline by way of an email sent to the Judge Pro Tem.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and then email the proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Travis Siegel, Esq. 562-645-4145 of Siegel Law for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN W. PIERCE, M.D. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

Defendant John W. Pierce, M.D.'s demurrer is overruled. The demurrer to the first cause of action for negligence/willful misconduct is overruled. The claim does not need to be segregated into two causes of action because it is a single tort. (See Berkley v. Dowds (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 518, 526 ["[Willful misconduct] is not a separate tort, but simply an aggravated form of negligence" (internal quotations omitted)]; Doe v. United States Youth Soccer Assn., Inc. (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 1118, 1140 [same].) For willful misconduct, plaintiff must allege (1) actual or constructive knowledge of the peril to be apprehended, (2) actual or constructive knowledge that injury is probable, as opposed to a possible, result of the danger, and (3) conscious failure to act to avoid the peril. (Id.) Here, plaintiff's allegations support the claim. (Complaint, 30-47.)

The demurrer to the second cause of action for elder abuse is overruled. The above adduced allegations support the elder abuse cause of action. "Abuse of an elder" includes "Physical abuse, neglect, abandonment, isolation, abduction, or other treatment with resulting physical harm or pain or mental suffering" and "The deprivation by a care custodian of goods or services that are necessary to avoid physical harm or mental suffering." (Welf. & Inst. Code, 15610.07(a)(1),(2); see also Welf. & Inst. Code, 15610.57(b)(2) [defining "neglect" to include "Failure to provide medical care for physical and mental needs."].) In this case, plaintiff alleges that decedent's low sodium levels and condition showed that decedent needed to be transferred back to the hospital immediately and defendant recklessly or maliciously withheld that care. (Compl. 30-31, 33-34.) A doctor that opposes hospitalization over several days where circumstances indicate it is medically necessary could be liable for neglect. (Mack v. Soung (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 966, 973, disapproved on other grounds, Winn v. Pioneer Medical Group, Inc. (2016) 63 Cal.4th 148 ["We have no trouble concluding that a doctor who . . . opposes [a patient's] hospitalization where circumstances indicate it is medically necessary . . . commits neglect within the meaning of the [Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act"]; see also, e.g., Sababin v. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 89-90 [reversing summary adjudication on dependent adult abuse cause of action where there were triable issues as to whether rehabilitation center's employees were guilty of reckless, oppressive or malicious neglect because there was evidence that they failed to follow plaintiff's care plan, thereby withholding necessary medical care].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Travis Siegel, Esq. 562-645-4145 of Siegel Law for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN W. PIERCE M.D'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'SCOMPLAINT.

Defendant John W. Pierce, M.D.'s motion to strike plaintiff's request for punitive damages is granted as to Paragraph 64 of the complaint but is otherwise denied as to the claim for punitive damages in the prayer for relief in the complaint. Plaintiff did not comply with the prefiling requirement of Code of Civil Procedure, 425.13. However, such compliance is not necessary where a party pleads a viable elder abuse claim. (See Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 790 ["Where the gravamen of an action is violation of the Elder Abuse Act, Central Pathology's rationale for applying section 425.13 to the common law intentional torts at issue in that case does not obtain."].) The motion to strike the claim for attorney's fees is denied because such relief can be available to the prevailing party on an elder abuse claim. (See Welf. & Insts. Code, 15657(a).) The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to 44-47. Those allegations relate to Medical Board disciplinary proceedings against Dr. Pierce concerning his treatment of other patients, and the Court strikes them as "improper" or "irrelevant" per Code of Civil Procedure, 436(a).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
